title: Module 1
content:
Introduction to Signals & Systems:  Elementary Signals, Classification of
signals, Basic operations on signals.
Concept of System, Classification, Properties of Systems-Linearity, Time
invariance, Causality, Stability.
Linear Time Invariant (LTI) Systems – Convolution sum, Impulse response,
Difference Equation representation of LTI Systems

title: Module 2
content:
Frequency Domain Approaches: Discrete Time Fourier Transform (DTFT),
Discrete Fourier Transform (DFT)-Properties.
Review of Laplace Transform, Region of Convergence (ROC), Properties of
ROC, Transfer function, Stability and causality conditions. Analysis of LTI
systems using Laplace transforms.
Sampling of continuous time signals (definition and concept only), Sampling
theorem for lowpass signals, aliasing.
Basic
Elements of a Digital Signal Processing (DSP) system, Typical DSP
applications.

title: Module 3
content:
Analog Communication: Block diagram of a communication system, Need
for modulation.
Principle of Amplitude Modulation (AM) – Frequency spectrum – power
relations – generation of AM – DSB, DSB/SC, SSB.
Angle Modulation - Frequency and Phase modulation - Theory and
generation of Frequency Modulation (FM).
Super heterodyne receivers (AM and FM).

title: Module 4
content:
Digital Communication: Sampling Process-Pulse modulation Techniques-
PAM, PWM and PPM, Principles of digital communication & advantages –
PCM encoder and decoder, Applications of data communication. Digital
modulation schemes – ASK, FSK, PSK.
Suggestion on Project Topics
